reasoning: Large sprite (lower-right) shows multiple clear AI tells. (1) GRADIENT SHADING: orange->white->green transitions on the bow body use intermediate blended tones rather than a strict 2-3 hard-tone pixel-art palette — typical AI diffusion smoothing artifact. (2) AA EDGES: bow boundary pixels show partial-alpha blending, especially on the upper-right limb tip and the string endpoints; clean pixel art should have hard 1px edges. (3) COLOR BLEED: orange fringe pixels bleed into the white highlight core and the green inner zone without a clean palette separation, consistent with neural upscaling/generation. (4) INCONSISTENT OUTLINES: outline thickness alternates between 1px and 2px along the bow limbs with no consistent intentional taper logic. Small sprite (upper-left) is cleaner but shares the same AA-edge issue on its curves. The combination of gradient shading + AA + color bleed across both sprites is characteristic of AI-generated pixel art rather than hand-authored work.
